steps.title: Determine distribution structure and calculate sum changes
steps.content:
With 27 numbers, median 33, and mean 33, the sum is $27 \times 33 = 891$.

Since the median is at the 14th position: 13 values are $< 33$, one value $= 33$, and 13 values are $> 33$.
The transformation adds $7 \times 13 = 91$ from values above 33 and subtracts $7 \times 13 = 91$ from values below 33, for a net change of zero.

The sum stays 891, so the mean stays 33.
Choices B (Mean) and C (Sum) are the same in both data sets.

steps.title: Verify the median stays at the 14th position
steps.content:
Relative order is preserved: values $< 33$ stay $< 33$ (even after subtracting 7), and values $> 33$ stay $> 33$ (even after adding 7). The element at the 14th position was 33 and remains 33.
Choice A (Median) is the same in both data sets.

steps.title: Standard deviation increases due to increased deviations from the mean
steps.content:
Standard deviation measures spread from the mean (33). For the new data set:
  • If original value $x < 33$: new deviation is $(x - 7) - 33 = (x - 33) - 7$, which is more negative
  • If original value $x > 33$: new deviation is $(x + 7) - 33 = (x - 33) + 7$, which is more positive
Every value moves farther from the mean, so all squared deviations increase. Variance and standard deviation both increase, making them different in the new data set. That matches choice D — D is correct.
